name: TableTopBar
description: Toolbar above a data table. Left slot hosts discovery / refinement controls (search, filter trigger); right slot hosts row-level actions (primary CTA, overflow). An optional filters row toggles open below the main row to host sort + filter chips.

props.name: slotLeft
props.description: Left slot content (search, icon buttons, etc.)

props.name: showFilters
props.description: Whether to show the filters row

states.rationale: TableTopBar is a passive layout container. Interactive behavior (hover/pressed/focus) lives on the composed children (SearchField, Button, FilterChip, Dropdown, etc.). The toolbar itself only positions content.

keyboard.Tab: Move focus to the next control inside the toolbar (natural tab order across slots)
keyboard.Shift+Tab: Move focus to the previous control inside the toolbar

accessibility.focusManagement: Natural tab order. The toolbar root carries role="toolbar" + aria-label. The Filter icon-Button in slotLeft should expose aria-expanded + aria-controls pointing at the filters row when it controls the row.

propertyMapping.note: Figma boolean toggles the left slot; in code, presence of the slotLeft ReactNode drives rendering.
Figma slot for the left content; pass SearchField + Filter icon Button (and other discovery controls) here.
Figma boolean inside slotLeftContent toggles the canonical Filter icon button. In code, the consumer composes the filter trigger inside slotLeft.
Figma slot for the right content; pass a primary Button + overflow icon-Button here.
Figma boolean inside slotRightContent toggles the canonical primary Button. In code, the consumer composes it inside slotRight.
Figma boolean inside slotRightContent toggles the canonical overflow icon-Button. In code, compose it inside slotRight.
Figma boolean toggles the secondary filters row. In code, also requires `filters` ReactNode to be provided.
Figma secondary row content (sort chip, separator, filter chips, add-filter trigger).

useWhen: Above any data table that needs search, filtering, and row-level actions in one consistent surface
Inbox / list view headers that mix search with primary action(s)
Settings or admin tables where the secondary filter row is toggled by a Filter icon button in the left slot
doNotUseFor: Page-level primary headings → use a semantic <h1> outside the table region
Form actions → use a form footer with a ButtonGroup

knownDeviations.description: Spec mentions --dls-color-component-table-top-bar-* tokens but they do not exist in tokens.json. The CSS uses semantic surface/border/spacing tokens directly. Backfilling deferred.

The Figma component embeds canonical default slot content (Search + Filter icon-button on the left; primary Button + overflow on the right; full Filters row with sample chips). In code, those are slot defaults the consumer must compose explicitly — the React component is purely slot-based.
